RT by @spendwiselyx: 💳✨ One of the best things about all Amex credit cards is that almost every type of spend counts towards milestone benefits. And that makes Amex cards especially useful for categories where many other cards give little or no rewards — like ⛽ fuel, 🏛️ government payments, 🛡️ insurance, 💰 NPS contributions etc.

👉 The Amex Reserve Credit Card offers a monthly milestone benefit where you get a ₹1,000 gift card (choose from BigBasket 🛒, Tata CliQ 🛍️, MakeMyTrip ✈️, PVR 🎬, BookMyShow 🍿) on ₹50,000 monthly spend.

And the best part? Even here, there are no spend exclusions for milestone qualification. 👏

Here’s how the rewards stack up 👇

🔹 Base rewards:
1 Reward Point per ₹50 spend = 2 Points per ₹100 spend
Assuming 1 point = ₹0.50 value ➝ approx 1% valueback 💰

🔹 Monthly milestone:
₹1,000 gift card on ₹50,000 spend ➝ Additional 2% valueback 🎁

➡️ Total = Approx 3% valueback on regular spending

🔹 Use Amex Multiplier portal:
Buy gift cards of popular brands through Amex Multiplier and earn 2X additional points 🚀

➡️ Extra ~2% valueback
➡️ Total becomes up to 5% valueback 🔥

So effectively:

⛽ Fuel, Insurance, Government Payments, NPS & Utilities
✅ No base rewards but eligible for milestone benefits
➡️ Approx 2% valueback through milestone rewards

🛒 Regular spends
✅ 1% base rewards + 2% milestone
➡️ Approx 3% valueback

🎯 Amex Multiplier spends
✅ 1% base + 2% milestone + 2% extra
➡️ Approx 5% valueback

Apart from rewards, Amex Reserve also includes premium lifestyle benefits ✨

🎁 Accor Explorer Membership every year
🍽️ Taj Epicure Membership every year
🥂 EazyDiner Prime Membership every year
🛫 12 Domestic Lounge Accesses/year (2 per quarter)
🌍 2 International Lounge Accesses/year + Free Priority Pass
⛳ Complimentary Golf Games every month (subject to ₹50,000 spend in previous month)

🎉 Plus, if you apply through referral on or before 31st May, you can get 19,000 Amex Membership Rewards points (worth approx ₹9,500 or potentially much higher depending on redemption) 🎁

🔗 Referral link (if interested in applying):
https://bit.ly/4dG5tqE

If you frequently stay at Accor 🏨 or Taj 🏨, this card may be a useful proposition, considering the joining benefits, annual memberships and other premium lifestyle benefits. ✨

items.title:
💳📉 Is this the real reason behind the sharp drop in credit card reward rates, reduced benefits, and the flood of devaluation announcements lately? 🤔🔥 👇👇👇 See the newspaper clipings from today’s ET 👇👇👇

Here’s an interesting perspective 👇

👉 Credit card rewards 🎁 and premium benefits ✈️🏨 are largely funded by the interest paid by credit revolvers — users who don’t pay their full credit card bill on time and carry forward outstanding balances 💸

👉 Such users often pay 45–48% annual interest 😳💰 on outstanding amounts, sometimes calculated from the transaction date. Historically, this has been one of the largest revenue streams for card issuers 🏦

👉 Banks also earn from:
✔️ Annual fees 💳  
✔️ Late payment charges ⚠️  
✔️ EMI interest 📆  
✔️ Forex markup 🌍  
✔️ Other fees & penalties 💵  

…but interest from revolving balances has traditionally been a major profit engine 🚀

👉 Over the last few years, the percentage of credit revolvers reportedly dropped from around 44% to 22% 📊⬇️ — almost half 😮

👉 Fewer revolvers = fewer people paying massive interest 💸 = lower profitability from revolving balances 📉 = potentially fewer rewards & benefits 🎁❌

👉 One reason - Awareness 🧠📚

More users now see credit cards as reward optimisation tools 🎯💳 instead of short-term borrowing instruments 💰

More people are:
✅ Paying bills in full every month  
✅ Maximising cashback & rewards 🎁  
✅ Converting spends into flights ✈️, hotels 🏨 & luxury experiences 🌴  

…instead of paying interest 😅

👉 This trend affects entry-level cards the most, because historically a larger share of revolvers came from this segment.

That may explain why we increasingly see:

📉 Lower cashback rates  
🚫 More exclusions  
🔒 Tighter reward conditions  
💔 Frequent devaluations  
🧾 Spend thresholds everywhere  

👉 Super-premium cards 💎✨ often operate differently.

Banks know these customers are less likely to revolve balances, but more likely to seek luxury experiences such ad:

✈️ Air travel
🏨 Luxury stays  
🍽️ Fine dining benefits  
🌍 Premium experiences & lounges
🚘 Concierge & lifestyle privileges  

In many cases, issuers may not earn significant direct profits from these cards. Instead, they use them to attract affluent customers 💼💰 and cross-sell:

📈 Wealth management  
💵 Investments  
🛡️ Insurance  
🏦 Premium banking products  
💎 High-margin financial services  

👉 That’s perhaps why in premium cards we are seeing higher eligibility criteria 🚪⬆️ and stricter entry barriers… rather than outright devaluations 📋

Of course, devaluations can also be driven by:

⚖️ Regulatory changes  
💰 Rising reward costs  
🏦 Issuer profitability pressures  
📊 Competition  
🌎 Changing spending behaviour  

So… what’s your view? 🤔👇

Is the decline in credit revolvers one of the biggest reasons behind shrinking rewards? Or are there other factors driving the wave of devaluations? 💬

As per this news article, the trend is going to continue in near future. So, would we see more such devaluations 🤔🤔🤔

Share your views in comments 👇👇👇

🌿✨ If anyone is considering a Taj redemption, do consider Taj Alibaug. I personally find it one of the most relaxing weekend getaways around Mumbai. 🏡💚

I first stayed here in Feb’21, when it was operated as Radisson, and visited again last month (Apr’26) after it became a Taj property.

The property is massive, surrounded by lush greenery 🌳, peaceful landscapes, and even mango trees loaded with fruits 🥭. The vibe is calm, serene, and perfect for slowing down from city life.

🚢 Pairing the staycation with the M2M Ferry makes the trip even better. The drive from the ferry terminal to Alibaug is beautiful, with greenery on both sides of the road (though the last ~4 km leading to the property is in poor condition for last 5 years at least😅).

But once you enter the resort… it feels like a different world ✨

You can spend your time enjoying:
🏊 Large swimming pool
🚴 Cycling around the property
⛳ Mini golf course
🎮 Indoor/outdoor games
🍽️ Multiple restaurants including multi-cuisine & specialty dining
🌿 Long peaceful walks amid nature
😌 Simply doing nothing and relaxing

During both our stays, we spent 2 nights, and I genuinely feel this property deserves at least a 2-night stay to fully experience and enjoy everything it offers.

If you have Taj vouchers, NeuCoins, Epicure benefits, or are planning a Taj redemption, this property is worth considering for a perfect weekend escape from Mumbai ❤️
